reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by sending a data column sidecar with >max_blobs_per_block KzG commitments (e.g., 100 commitments when limit is 6/9/12), causing Prysm nodes to process and store oversized columns leading to memory/CPU exhaustion and DoS. Severity downgraded from High to Medium: single-client bug affecting ~31% of network (Prysm market share), which exceeds 5% but not 33% threshold.
